method_atom: A constrained maximum-likelihood estimation of a latent aggregate quantity that is only partially observable because a parent entity splits into several products, some of whose degrees of freedom escape measurement. Rather than relying on a limiting approximation that is valid only in a small fraction of cases, the unmeasured variables are inferred by minimizing a likelihood function over the full physically allowed configuration space, subject to consistency and conservation constraints that link the unobserved and observed components.
object_atom: The invariant mass of a heavy resonance (a Z/gamma* or Higgs boson) that decays into a pair of tau leptons, whose subsequent decays emit multiple neutrinos that escape the detector, reconstructed for collider events at the Tevatron (CDF) and LHC.
brief_reason: The constrained-likelihood inference of a hidden global quantity from partially observed products carries across fields; the tau-pair resonance mass with missing neutrinos at particle colliders is the domain-specific object.
